conv1-open.speaker: me

text.en: Excuse me, you look like there's something very serious happening right now.

conv1-open.context: Mongolian backend developer smoking outside. Low-energy day, matching her state. From [IS2SoUgqDLE].

text.en: No?
Oh, okay, cool. What's up? You've got a very soft voice. Did you party too much last night?
Yeah. I was working all day. I don't look nice. Everybody else does.
Ah, well, you must look amazing when you've had rest. What's your name?
I'm from Mongolia.
Are you from Mongolia? The face structure, yeah. And you're studying here, I guess?
No. At this point, I think I prefer home. I can make more money at home. The tax here is really bad, 37%.
It's tough to make it, isn't it? No matter where you are. I'm Australian, but I mostly live here and in Portugal.
It's not so common for me that somebody comes to talk.
Does that happen in Mongolia at all? Do people talk to each other?
Only in the bar.
Only in the bar, yeah. I think mostly men are a bit scared of women. I don't know why. You don't seem scary.
How old are you?
Forty-four. Do I look older or younger?
No, no. Thirty-seven.
No, thank you. I am the age that I am. You can be sexy at any age, right?
Twenty-five. I'm still building my life and that's it. I still have to work on weekends doing part-time jobs. My main job is I'm a backend developer.
Well, that's useful stuff. That's not going out of style. Do you not worry that AI is going to take your job?
I do. And I still use AI at work.

Oh, you're part of the problem. You're making yourself obsolete. Well, milk it while you can before the machines take over. So, in amongst your very busy schedule, do you have time to have some fun, ever?
Not today, but after six, after I finish work. Weekends, I want to go to the gym and sleep.
All right. Well, would you like to get a drink sometime when you're rested? Do you have WhatsApp?
Yeah. I'll just text you now.
Got it. Cool. All right, well nice to meet you. Take it easy.

conv2-open.speaker: me

text.en: Hi. How are you? I saw you walking by. I thought you looked really lovely. So here I am to say hi. What's your name?

conv2-open.context: Direct stop on the street, Budapest. Girl has a boyfriend. From [IS2SoUgqDLE].

text.en: Hi.
I'm James. Are you a local person?
Yes. I've been here about 10 years now.
Okay, that's very local. And what's the plan for your Sunday?
Actually, I'm meeting my boyfriend, so...
You met with him or you will meet with him? Did you have a big night last night?
Yes.
Yeah. You look a little bit dreamy, just like, am I still high or am I talking to myself? Good. Well, did you have fun? Was it worth it?
Yeah.
Good. Cool. All right, have yourself an awesome day. See you later.

conv3-open.speaker: me

text.en: Hello. You okay? You've got kind of secret agent vibes going on here. Are you a little tired?

conv3-open.context: Hungarian girl with limited English, sitting on a bench. Extended approach leading to number. From [IS2SoUgqDLE].

text.en: I don't understand. I speak a little English.
I'll speak a bit slowly. What is your name? I'm James.
Hi. Good handshake. Strong.
And you have the day off today? No work today? What do you usually do?
Difficult to explain. I don't speak. I understand you a little bit, but I don't speak.
I guess you don't have many opportunities to practice with a native speaker, right? Also, I am Australian, so my accent is maybe strong. What is your plan now? You do not have a plan?
No plan.
Okay. Well, I will go now to work with my friend. But maybe we can get a drink later. Would you like? You understand?
Okay. Yeah, I think we will need Google Translate.
We need a chaperone on the date. It doesn't matter. What do you think? We can try. So, if you give me your WhatsApp number, I will text you.
I have a WhatsApp and Viber.
Viber I can do, yes. All right, excellent. I will text you later then. Have a good relax. We'll see you soon. Bye.

conv4-open.speaker: me

text.en: Hello. How are you? I just saw you walk by. You looked lovely and summery. I thought I'd introduce myself. What's your name?

conv4-open.context: Hungarian girl heading to a book festival, brief interaction. From [IS2SoUgqDLE].

text.en: Hi.
I'm James. You're not Hungarian then, right?
No, I am. And I'm walking to see if there's still the book festival.
There definitely is. I was there before and there are many people. What kind of books do you like to read?
Maybe drama. Real stories. I like Paul Auster.
Paul Auster? I don't know him.
Well, his books are not really about romance. But actually I have to go see this.
You're in a big rush for books. Understood. Okay. See you later.

conv5-open.speaker: me

text.en: Hello. How are you? Are you a tourist? This is the first time you've seen the Parisi? It looks lovely.

conv5-open.context: Friendly Hungarian student from a small town, studying special needs education. From [IS2SoUgqDLE].

text.en: No, I'm Hungarian. It is nice.
It's good that even though you live in a city, you can still see the little pieces of beauty everywhere. And what are you doing today?
Today I have to study. I go back home and study for my exam.
So you just had a little shopping break? What are you studying?
Special needs education.
That must be very challenging and also rewarding work. I'm from Australia, but I live here most of the time. What's your name?
Hi.
I'm James. Nice to meet you. You have a very friendly vibe. A lot of Hungarians can be a bit grumpy, right?
Yes. I'm from a small town, about 100 kilometres away. I think people in small towns are more friendly.

You're a country girl. It makes sense when you're young to go explore and take advantage of opportunities in a city. I did the same. Now I live half the time on a farm in Portugal. An olive farm. And why did you decide to study special needs education?
Last year I was in Germany as a volunteer, and I worked in a special school. I just like it.

Yeah, it's fun with people who think differently, who have an unusual way of experiencing life. All right, you're going this way? Would you like to get a coffee sometime? You seem very lovely. Do you have WhatsApp?
Yes. I have an exam coming soon.
Cool. I'm James, if you don't remember. Have a lovely day. Good luck with your studies.

conv6-open.speaker: me

text.en: Hello. How are you? That painting is nearly as big as you. Did you steal it from an art gallery?

conv6-open.context: Adorable Hungarian art student carrying a large painting. Long interaction with hand-holding moment. From [03m9yo-ikPc].

text.en: Yes, I can. It's a half-done picture.
What will the other half be? What kind of art? Impressionist, surrealist, or something?
Symbolism.
I don't know what symbolism is. Explain it to me. Like little things that are symbolic? So what, for example?
It will come to you when you get inspiration. You will get inspiration and then you will choose the symbol. Something like this.
You're so cute. It's okay, we will speak slowly. You'll remember your English. What is your name? My name is James.
Nice to meet you.
You are adorable. Do you know this word? Adorable means very cute.
Oh. Thank you.
And you are Hungarian, yeah. You have very beautiful eyes. Like hypnotic, do you know this word?
Yeah, I know hypnotic. And do you like my eyes? Are they also hypnotic?
I don't know. So where are you going right now?
The museum. I have a lesson. An art lesson. I study graphic design and illustration, but now I paint.
Today you paint? Tomorrow you conquer the world? I'm from Australia, but I live in Budapest. And you like Budapest?
I am not from Budapest. I'm from a little town.
So you think people in the small towns are more friendly? But you're a naturally friendly person, right? You understand 50% of what I say?
50-50 is all right. We can work on that. What time is your class? Now? Okay well listen, would you like to get a cup of coffee with me sometime? I would like to take you out.
Maybe.
All right, well let me solve the maybe. Why don't I get your contact, I can text you, and eventually we'll do the thing. You have WhatsApp?
I have Viber and Messenger.
I think I still have Viber. Do you remember my name?
James.
And maybe we can meet this weekend? Do you also have Instagram? Let's do that as well.
Yes. This is for you.
A chocolate egg? All right, well I hope you have a good day and you get inspiration to find your symbols. I will text you and when you come back to Budapest we meet. Bye.

conv7-open.speaker: me

text.en: Hello. How are you? I'm not selling anything. You've got that look of, why am I here? Do you know why I'm here? To tell you you look wonderful today and to meet you.

conv7-open.context: Hungarian travel agent with headphones on, listening to YouTube. Leads to dirty talk banter. From [J02LapVhOyM].

text.en: Oh, thank you so much.
Are you on the phone or you're listening to a podcast? What's your name? How do you do? I'm James.
James, where are you from?
I'm Australian. But I live here, let's say, half the time. I have my own business, so I can work from wherever, but I have my media team here.
Oh, that's really wonderful. And are you Hungarian?
Your eyes are quite similar to mine. Beautiful greeny grey. It's pretty rare to find, right?
I've been living here in Budapest for a year. I'm from Western Hungary, but I really like Budapest actually.
Yeah, it's like, it's a little bit dirty, but a little bit beautiful, there's a lot going on. And what are you doing in life?
I work at a travel agency. I study tourism and hospitality. But later, after my 30s, I want to start my own business.
After your 30s? You've got a specific timeline. In your 20s you want more experience first? In everything? Work, pleasure?
Yeah, in everything. I want to travel a lot and see the world. I want to live abroad. Spain, Italy or France.
Is that because the Hungarian men are not your style?
My husband will be Hungarian, you know? I just don't like talking to a man in Hungarian.
What's it like to do dirty talk in Hungarian? Does it sound good?
I don't like dirty talking in Hungarian. At all.
So do you switch to English for dirty talk, even with a Hungarian?
In Hungarian, I don't like these expressions, even from a man. Most young Hungarian girls just want to live abroad. Hungarians are really negative, not welcoming. Cold personality.
Maybe that's a hangover from the Soviet Union days. But you're very friendly and open. You have a nice smile too. It doesn't look fake or anything.
Thank you. And what are you doing now? I have to go buy some groceries. And after that I have a meeting with my friends. We are girls so we really like...
You need to gossip. Do you have any juicy gossip this time?
Maybe.
Excellent. I'd like to take you out sometime if you'd like that. Can I get your WhatsApp?
Okay. Yeah, exactly.
I will text you on WhatsApp and when we have time we can meet. Take it easy. Bye.

conv8-open.speaker: me

text.en: Hello. Are you waiting for a tram? Cool. In that case, we have two minutes to talk. What's your name? I'm James. How are you doing?

conv8-open.context: Hungarian girl at a tram stop. Playful banter about life falling apart, ends with boyfriend reveal. From [WSFSpbFCPZo].

text.en: Hi. What's that accent? Are you French?
I wish.
I'm Hungarian.
Be proud of your nation. What about your amazing qualities? Just give me one, we don't have much time. Are you a nice person?
Yeah. I can be very nice and I can be very not nice.
Very nice and very nasty. Only if somebody deserves it, I hope.
Where are you from?
Australia. Yeah, but I live here half the time. Life coaching. Helping people with confidence and public speaking. Do you have your shit together or is it all a mess?
I think it's all collapsing right now so I'm going to start working on it.
The good thing about those times is that they open many possibilities.
I just got a motivation quote today from this app. Something like when one door closes, everything opens.
Yeah, it's true. Is this your tram? Okay, I know we don't know each other. Would you like to get a coffee sometime?
Yeah, but I'm in a relationship.
Then you shouldn't have a coffee with me. It's very dangerous. Take it easy. I hope the chaos forms into something that was meant to be. Peace out.

conv9-open.speaker: me

text.en: Excuse me. Hi. I didn't mean to startle you, but you look lovely. I thought I'd meet you. What's your name? I'm James.

conv9-open.context: Young Hungarian university student studying international relations. Cold day, walking approach. Successful number close. From [WSFSpbFCPZo].

text.en: Australia? Why? It is very far away.

And we have lots of cool animals and nice beaches and chill people. No, I live here let's say half the time. I have an online business so I can work wherever I want. You're definitely Hungarian, I can tell from the accent and the name. You don't have the very strong one. Yours is quite sweet and nice.
I spent my summers in the United States, so maybe I picked up some accent there.
No, you still have a Hungarian accent, but it's a good one. It's nice. I'm not gonna follow you. I'll let you go shortly. What's the plan, you're going to university? What are you studying?
International relations. It's my first semester. I found a new place.
So you're still just figuring it out. Are you business or mathematically minded?
I'm trying to be.
All right. Well, I'm gonna head back because it's cold. It's lovely to chat to you. Would you like to get a cup of tea sometime? Do you have a WhatsApp or something?
Yeah, okay.
All right, cool. Well, I'm gonna be traveling for a week or so but I'll text you when I get back. Take it easy. See you. Bye.

conv10-open.speaker: me

text.en: Excuse me lady, hello. You look amazing.

conv10-open.context: Curvy Irish tourist visiting Budapest solo. Gems on teeth, Middle Eastern heritage, product manager. From [N2UbTTfCfqg].

text.en: Oh, thank you so much.
Yeah, you're very welcome. What are you doing today?
I'm just going to the shopping centre. How about you?
Getting some sunshine. Are you British? Irish? You do not look Irish.
No, because my parents are from the Middle East while I grew up in Ireland.
It's interesting hearing that voice coming out of clearly not an Irish face. Do you feel Irish?
I do, yeah, because I've been there all my life.
Are you funny? Because all Irish people are really funny.
Yeah, I'd like to think so. The British are funny as well, no?
The British tend to be more sarcastic, whereas the Irish are a bit sillier. What are you doing? Do you live here or are you travelling?
No, I just came here on holidays. Solo today, my friends come tomorrow.
Have you been to the bathhouses yet?
Not yet. I just landed today. I heard mixed reviews. Some people say it's smelly.

It smells a bit like sulphur because it's natural. But the Szechenyi one, the outdoor one, is awesome. You get to lie in a hot bath in old Austro-Hungarian surrounds. In this weather it's better to go at sunset.
Oh, that's true. I'll take that into consideration. It was nice meeting you. I'm going to head home because I have a lot to do today.
How long are you here for?
I'm here until Wednesday.
All right, cool. Would you like to grab a drink sometime?
Yeah, I'm down.
All right, cool. Have you got WhatsApp? I presume? You will have to remind me of your exotic and exquisite name.
I'll type it in. There you go.
All right. Well, I'll text you. Take it easy. Have a wonderful day.

conv11-open.speaker: me

text.en: Hello, you look super cool today. Can I walk with you for one moment? I'm not selling anything.

conv11-open.context: Real estate developer going through a divorce. Walking approach, blue outfit banter, number close. From [kyBKT_uIREA].

text.en: Thank you. Okay. What's your name?
I'm James. Nice to meet you. Are you dressed up for something specifically or are you just a very colourful person?
This week I always use blue.
Oh, it's blue week? Does your outfit match your mood or are you just experimenting?
Sometimes I'm so different each day. And I really like blue.
I'll walk with you one more block. So you can ask me anything. I'll be very honest.
Where are you from?
I'm Australian. I live in Budapest most of the time. I've been here on and off for eight years. I have a company here. What do you do?
I have a real estate company. I work alone. I sell properties. Hotels, actually.
You're selling hotels? So you're doing big deals. It's cool to be independent, right? To build something by yourself. Are you a good negotiator? You know when to flatter and when to push?
Yeah. It's difficult to deal with investors sometimes. A lot of things. That's why I work alone.
Well, you seem quite interesting. I like your style. Would you like to get a drink sometime when you're not busy?
You know, I don't want to tell you this, but I have a hard situation. It's a difficult part of my personal life.
Are you going through a divorce or something?
Yes. It started three years ago and next month it will be finished. My lawyer thinks. And I look like a younger woman, but I'm not 20 years old. I'm 33.

I'm 45. So, you know, we both look younger than we are. I understand it's a complicated part of your life and you probably want a break from men. I'm actually going away to Portugal for a few weeks. How about I get your number, and when I come back and you've settled things, we can meet for a cup of tea and see how things go?
Maybe I can give you my number. But do you have Instagram?

I do but that's for kids. I won't blow up your phone. I will text you once when I come back. I'm not the kind of guy that's going to hassle a woman. If you want to meet, good. If not, I respect your decision.
Okay. Do you remember my name? James. Very good.
WhatsApp is easiest. Put your number in. I will send you a text. All right, lovely to meet you. Take care.

conv12-open.speaker: me

text.en: Hello. You look cool. Are you a bit stoned right now?

conv12-open.context: Hungarian hippie girl with flowers in her hair, sitting in a park. Deep conversation about unconditional love, the matrix, art. From [2h9wq53QIQk].

text.en: You are? Yeah.
Yeah, I thought so. And you've got flowers in your hair, so you're just enjoying that zone. I'm not stoned, but my name is James. I'm a previous stoner, so I don't judge at all.
Good, good. I'm from here. Do you meditate?
Yeah, I run a life coaching business. Meditation, tantra. Do you meditate?
My main intention was to get some holiness to myself. Some days ago I had a big fight with my love. So now I want to get back to the state of arriving to myself. I want to go somewhere in nature.
I see. My grandmother was a Hungarian lady, so I have some part of my blood is Hungarian. But I don't know the language. It's very difficult.
I was living in the Netherlands. But my roots come from this soil, so I had to come back. And now I feel more free here.
You feel more free in Hungary than the Netherlands? Interesting. You would be the first person to say that. So your plan is to go over the bridge and up to the mountain?
Yeah. Do you want to walk with me?
I can walk a little bit. I have to go back to my friend soon, but I'll walk with you for a few minutes. What do you like doing in life? What's something you're interested in?
Too many ideas. I feel I have an interesting way to learn about unconditional love. Hopefully this brings me somewhere.
Well, it certainly will help. Although unconditional love, do you think that exists really? I think there's always some kind of conditions. Maybe certain saints, but humans have agendas.
Thank you. You say you love people and I like to hear it.
Yeah. I mean, not everyone all the time, but in general I'm a humanist. Are you an artistic person?
That brings me joy. But to work in the structure of, you know, the matrix. The matrix makes me nervous.

The matrix, yes. It's difficult to escape. I think I'm pretty close to escaped. I never worked for anybody else and I always did the things I wanted. If you can't escape it, you can learn to surf the matrix. Use it to your advantage.
What is your business?
I teach tantra and meditation and life coaching. I would love to go for a walk with you in nature, but I actually have to go visit my friends now.
I don't have to worry about it because I need some self-time. But I'm really happy you were my companion for this short meeting. It was beautiful.
Nice to see you. Would you like to meet some other time? Do you have WhatsApp?
Yeah. We can do WhatsApp.
All right. I wish you a wonderful, peaceful day. We'll see you soon. Bye.
